    87→# =============================================================================
    88→# Data models
    89→# =============================================================================
    90→
    91→@dataclass(frozen=True)
    92→class Scope:
    93→    """Represents a scope as a flat string-to-string mapping."""
    94→
    95→    _data: dict[str, str]
    96→
    97→    def __post_init__(self):
    98→        object.__setattr__(self, '_data', dict(self._data))
    99→
   100→    def keys(self):
   101→        return self._data.keys()
   102→
   103→    def values(self):
   104→        return self._data.values()
   105→
   106→    def items(self):
   107→        return self._data.items()
   108→
   109→    def get(self, key: str, default=None):
   110→        return self._data.get(key, default)
   111→
   112→    def __getitem__(self, key: str):
   113→        return self._data[key]
   114→
   115→    def __eq__(self, other):
   116→        if not isinstance(other, Scope):
   117→            return False
   118→        return self._data == other._data
   119→
   120→    def __hash__(self):
   121→        # Hash based on sorted items
   122→        return hash(tuple(sorted(self._data.items())))
   123→
   124→    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, str]:
   125→        return dict(self._data)
   126→
   127→    @classmethod
   128→    def from_dict(cls, d: dict[str, str]) -> 'Scope':
   129→        if not isinstance(d, dict):
   130→            raise ValidationError('invalid_input', 'Scope must be a JSON object')
   131→        for k, v in d.items():
   132→            if not isinstance(k, str):
   133→                raise ValidationError('invalid_input', f'Scope key must be string, got {type(k).__name__}')
   134→            if not isinstance(v, str):
   135→                raise ValidationError('invalid_input', f'Scope value must be string, got {type(v).__name__}')
   136→        return cls(d)
   137→
   138→
   139→@dataclass(frozen=True)
   140→class IncludeRef:
   141→    """Reference to another config."""
   142→
   143→    name: str
   144→    scope: Scope
   145→    version: int | None  # None means use current active
   146→
   147→    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
   148→        result = {
   149→            'name': self.name,
   150→            'scope': self.scope.to_dict(),
   151→        }
   152→        if self.version is not None:
   153→            result['version'] = self.version
   154→        return result
   155→
   156→    @classmethod
   157→    def from_dict(cls, d: dict[str, Any]) -> 'IncludeRef':
   158→        if not isinstance(d, dict):
   159→            raise ValidationError('invalid_input', 'Include reference must be a JSON object')
   160→
   161→        if 'name' not in d:
   162→            raise ValidationError('invalid_input', 'Include reference must have "name"')
   163→        if 'scope' not in d:
   164→            raise ValidationError('invalid_input', 'Include reference must have "scope"')
   165→
   166→        name = d['name']
   167→        if not isinstance(name, str) or not name:
   168→            raise ValidationError('invalid_input', 'Include name must be non-empty string')
   169→
   170→        scope = Scope.from_dict(d['scope'])
   171→        version = d.get('version')
   172→        if version is not None and version is not None:  # noqa: E711
   173→            if not isinstance(version, int) or version < 1:
   174→                raise ValidationError('invalid_input', 'Version must be positive integer')
   175→
   176→        return cls(name=name, scope=scope, version=version)
   177→
   178→
   179→@dataclass(frozen=True)
   180→class ConfigVersion:
   181→    """An immutable version of a config."""
   182→
   183→    name: str
   184→    scope: Scope
   185→    version: int
   186→    config: dict[str, Any]
   187→    includes: tuple[IncludeRef, ...] = ()
   188→    active: bool = False
   189→
   190→    def to_dict(self, include_config: bool = True) -> dict[str, Any]:
   191→        result = {
   192→            'name': self.name,
   193→            'scope': self.scope.to_dict(),
   194→            'version': self.version,
   195→            'active': self.active,
   196→        }
   197→        if include_config:
   198→            result['config'] = self.config
   199→            result['includes'] = [inc.to_dict() for inc in self.includes]
   200→        return result
   201→
   202→
   203→# =============================================================================
   204→# Storage
   205→# =============================================================================
   206→
   207→@dataclass
   208→class ConfigStorage:
   209→    """In-memory storage for configs. In production, would use a database."""
   210→
   211→    # (name, scope_hash) -> list of ConfigVersion sorted by version
   212→    _configs: dict[tuple[str, int], list[ConfigVersion]] = field(default_factory=dict)
   213→
   214→    def _scope_key(self, name: str, scope: Scope) -> tuple[str, int]:
   215→        """Generate a key for the (name, scope) pair."""
   216→        # Use hash of scope for dictionary key
   217→        scope_hash = hash(scope)
   218→        return (name, scope_hash)
   219→
   220→    def _get_versions(self, name: str, scope: Scope) -> list[ConfigVersion]:
   221→        """Get all versions for a (name, scope) pair."""
   222→        key = self._scope_key(name, scope)
   223→        return self._configs.get(key, [])
   224→
   225→    def create_version(
   226→        self,
   227→        name: str,
   228→        scope: Scope,
   229→        config: dict[str, Any],
   230→        includes: list[IncludeRef],
   231→        inherits_active: bool = False
   232→    ) -> ConfigVersion:
   233→        """Create a new immutable version."""
   234→        key = self._scope_key(name, scope)
   235→        versions = self._configs.get(key, [])
   236→
   237→        # Check max versions
   238→        if len(versions) >= MAX_VERSIONS_PER_SCOPE:
   239→            raise ConflictError('conflict', f'Maximum {MAX_VERSIONS_PER_SCOPE} versions reached for {name}')
   240→
   241→        # Determine next version number
   242→        next_version = len(versions) + 1
   243→
   244→        # Check idempotency - if identical request, return existing
   245→        # Two configs are identical if same name, scope, config, and includes
   246→        for v in versions:
   247→            if v.config == config and v.includes == tuple(includes):
   248→                # Return existing version, but ensure it's active
   249→                if not v.active:
   250→                    # Need to activate it
   251→                    object.__setattr__(v, 'active', True)
   252→                return v
   253→
   254→        # If inherits_active is True, inherit omitted fields from active version
   255→        if inherits_active and versions:
   256→            active = versions[-1]  # Last one is active
   257→            # Deep merge active.config into config (active wins for conflicts)
   258→            config = deep_merge(active.config, config)
   259→
   260→        # Deactivate all existing versions
   261→        for v in versions:
   262→            object.__setattr__(v, 'active', False)
   263→
   264→        # Create new version
   265→        new_version = ConfigVersion(
   266→            name=name,
   267→            scope=scope,
   268→            version=next_version,
   269→            config=config,
   270→            includes=tuple(includes),
   271→            active=True
   272→        )
   273→
   274→        # Store it
   275→        self._configs[key] = versions + [new_version]
   276→
   277→        return new_version
   278→
   279→    def get_version(self, name: str, scope: Scope, version: int) -> ConfigVersion:
   280→        """Get a specific version."""
   281→        versions = self._get_versions(name, scope)
   282→        for v in versions:
   283→            if v.version == version:
   284→                return v
   285→        raise NotFoundError('not_found', f'Version {version} not found for {name}')
   286→
   287→    def get_active(self, name: str, scope: Scope) -> ConfigVersion:
   288→        """Get the active version."""
   289→        versions = self._get_versions(name, scope)
   290→        for v in reversed(versions):
   291→            if v.active:
   292→                return v
   293→        raise NotFoundError('not_found', f'No active version for {name}')
   294→
   295→    def list_versions(self, name: str, scope: Scope) -> list[ConfigVersion]:
   296→        """List all versions for a (name, scope) pair."""
   297→        return list(self._get_versions(name, scope))
   298→
   299→    def activate_version(self, name: str, scope: Scope, version: int) -> ConfigVersion:
   300→        """Activate a specific version."""
   301→        versions = self._get_versions(name, scope)
   302→
   303→        target = None
   304→        for v in versions:
   305→            if v.version == version:
   306→                target = v
   307→                break
   308→
   309→        if target is None:
   310→            raise NotFoundError('not_found', f'Version {version} not found for {name}')
   311→
   312→        # Deactivate all others
   313→        for v in versions:
   314→            object.__setattr__(v, 'active', v.version == version)
   315→
   316→        return target
   317→
   318→    def rollback(self, name: str, scope: Scope, to_version: int) -> ConfigVersion:
   319→        """Rollback to an earlier version."""
   320→        versions = self._get_versions(name, scope)
   321→
   322→        # Check if to_version exists
   323→        target = None
   324→        for v in versions:
   325→            if v.version == to_version:
   326→                target = v
   327→                break
   328→
   329→        if target is None:
   330→            raise NotFoundError('not_found', f'Version {to_version} not found for {name}')
   331→
   332→        # Find current active
   333→        current_active = None
   334→        for v in reversed(versions):
   335→            if v.active:
   336→                current_active = v
   337→                break
   338→
   339→        if current_active is None:
   340→            raise NotFoundError('not_found', f'No active version for {name}')
   341→
   342→        # Only allow rollback to earlier or same version
   343→        if to_version > current_active.version:
   344→            raise ConflictError('conflict', f'Cannot rollback to version {to_version} (newer than active {current_active.version})')
   345→
   346→        return self.activate_version(name, scope, to_version)
   347→
   348→
   349→# =============================================================================
   350→# Deep merge with type checking
   351→# =============================================================================
   352→
   353→def deep_merge(base: dict[str, Any], override: dict[str, Any], path: str = '') -> dict[str, Any]:
   354→    """
   355→    Deep merge two dictionaries.
   356→    Override values take precedence.
   357→    Raises UnprocessableError on type conflicts.
   358→    """
   359→    result = dict(base)
   360→
   361→    for key, value in override.items():
   362→        current_path = f'{path}/{key}' if path else f'/{key}'
   363→
   364→        if key in result:
   365→            existing = result[key]
   366→
   367→            # Type conflict detection
   368→            if isinstance(existing, dict) and isinstance(value, dict):
   369→                # Both are dicts, recurse
   370→                result[key] = deep_merge(existing, value, current_path)
   371→            elif isinstance(existing, list) and isinstance(value, list):
   372→                # Both are lists, replace entirely (right wins)
   373→                result[key] = value
   374→            elif isinstance(existing, (str, int, float, bool, type(None))) and \
   375→                 isinstance(value, (str, int, float, bool, type(None))):
   376→                # Both are scalars, replace (right wins)
   377→                result[key] = value
   378→            else:
   379→                # Type conflict
   380→                raise UnprocessableError(
   381→                    'unprocessable',
   382→                    f'Type conflict at {current_path}: {type(existing).__name__} vs {type(value).__name__}',
   383→                    {'path': current_path}
   384→                )
   385→        else:
   386→            result[key] = value
   387→
   388→    return result
   389→
   390→
   391→# =============================================================================
   392→# Resolution engine
   393→# =============================================================================
   394→
   395→@dataclass
   396→class ResolutionNode:
   397→    """A node in the resolution graph."""
   398→
   399→    name: str
   400→    scope: Scope
   401→    version_used: int
   402→
   403→    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
   404→        return {
   405→            'name': self.name,
   406→            'scope': self.scope.to_dict(),
   407→            'version_used': self.version_used,
   408→        }
   409→
   410→
   411→def resolve_config(
   412→    storage: ConfigStorage,
   413→    name: str,
   414→    scope: Scope,
   415→    version: int | None,
   416→    visited: set[tuple[str, int, int]] | None = None
   417→) -> tuple[dict[str, Any], list[ResolutionNode]]:
   418→    """
   419→    Resolve a config with all includes applied.
   420→
   421→    Returns (resolved_config, resolution_graph).
   422→
   423→    Raises:
   424→        NotFoundError: If referenced config doesn't exist.
   425→        CycleDetectedError: If a cycle is detected.
   426→        UnprocessableError: If merge has type conflicts.
   427→    """
   428→    if visited is None:
   429→        visited = set()
   430→
   431→    # Check max depth
   432→    if len(visited) > MAX_INCLUDE_CHAIN:
   433→        raise UnprocessableError(
   434→            'unprocessable',
   435→            'Maximum include chain length exceeded',
   436→            {'reason': 'max_depth'}
   437→        )
   438→
   439→    # Get the config version
   440→    if version is None:
   441→        config_version = storage.get_active(name, scope)
   442→    else:
   443→        config_version = storage.get_version(name, scope, version)
   444→
   445→    # Create unique identifier for this config/version
   446→    config_id = (name, hash(scope), config_version.version)
   447→
   448→    # Check for cycles
   449→    if config_id in visited:
   450→        raise CycleDetectedError(
   451→            'cycle_detected',
   452→            f'Cycle detected involving {name}',
   453→            {'cycle': [n.to_dict() for n in visited]}
   454→        )
   455→
   456→    visited = visited | {config_id}
   457→
   458→    # Start with empty object
   459→    resolved = {}
   460→    graph = [ResolutionNode(
   461→        name=config_version.name,
   462→        scope=config_version.scope,
   463→        version_used=config_version.version
   464→    )]
   465→
   466→    # Process includes in order
   467→    for include_ref in config_version.includes:
   468→        # Determine which version to use
   469→        ref_version = include_ref.version
   470→        if ref_version is None:
   471→            # Use active version at resolution time
   472→            ref_version = storage.get_active(include_ref.name, include_ref.scope).version
   473→
   474→        # Recursively resolve
   475→        included_config, included_graph = resolve_config(
   476→            storage,
   477→            include_ref.name,
   478→            include_ref.scope,
   479→            ref_version,
   480→            visited
   481→        )
   482→
   483→        # Merge into accumulator
   484→        try:
   485→            resolved = deep_merge(resolved, included_config)
   486→        except UnprocessableError as e:
   487→            # Re-raise with more context
   488→            raise UnprocessableError(
   489→                'unprocessable',
   490→                e.message,
   491→                e.details
   492→            )
   493→
   494→        # Add to graph (without duplicates)
   495→        for node in included_graph:
   496→            node_id = (node.name, hash(node.scope), node.version_used)
   497→            if node_id not in { (n.name, hash(n.scope), n.version_used) for n in graph }:
   498→                graph.append(node)
   499→
   500→    # Finally, merge own config on top
   501→    try:
   502→        resolved = deep_merge(resolved, config_version.config)
   503→    except UnprocessableError as e:
   504→        raise UnprocessableError(
   505→            'unprocessable',
   506→            e.message,
   507→            e.details
   508→        )
   509→
   510→    return resolved, graph
   511→
   512→
   513→# =============================================================================
   514→# FastAPI Application
   515→# =============================================================================
   516→

    67→@dataclass(frozen=True)
    68→class IncludeRef:
    69→    """Reference to another config."""
    70→
    71→    name: str
    72→    scope: Scope
    73→    version: int | None  # None means use current active
    74→
    75→    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
    76→        result = {

    78→            'scope': self.scope.to_dict(),

    84→    @classmethod
    85→    def from_dict(cls, d: dict[str, Any]) -> 'IncludeRef':
    86→        if not isinstance(d, dict):
    87→            raise ValidationError('invalid_input', 'Include reference must be a JSON object')
    88→
    89→        if 'name' not in d:
    90→            raise ValidationError('invalid_input', 'Include reference must have "name"')
    91→        if 'scope' not in d:
    92→            raise ValidationError('invalid_input', 'Include reference must have "scope"')
    93→
    94→        name = d['name']
    95→        if not isinstance(name, str) or not name:
    96→            raise ValidationError('invalid_input', 'Include name must be non-empty string')
    97→
    98→        scope = Scope.from_dict(d['scope'])
    99→        version = d.get('version')
   100→        if version is not None:
   101→            if not isinstance(version, int) or version < 1:
   102→                raise ValidationError('invalid_input', 'Version must be positive integer')
   103→
   104→        return cls(name=name, scope=scope, version=version)

   107→@dataclass(frozen=True)
   108→class ConfigVersion:
   109→    """An immutable version of a config."""
   110→
   111→    name: str
   112→    scope: Scope
   113→    version: int
   114→    config: dict[str, Any]
   115→    includes: tuple[IncludeRef, ...] = ()
   116→    active: bool = False

   118→    def to_dict(self, include_config: bool = True) -> dict[str, Any]:
   119→        result = {

   121→            'scope': self.scope,
   122→            'version': self.version,

   135→@dataclass
   136→class ConfigStorage:
   137→    """In-memory storage for configs. In production, would use a database."""
   138→
   139→    # (name, scope_hash) -> list of ConfigVersion sorted by version
   140→    _configs: dict[tuple[str, int], list[ConfigVersion]] = field(default_factory=dict)
   141→
   142→    def _scope_key(self, name: str, scope: Scope) -> tuple[str, int]:
   143→        """Generate a key for the (name, scope) pair."""
   144→        return (name, scope_hash(scope))
   145→
   146→    def _get_versions(self, name: str, scope: Scope) -> list[ConfigVersion]:
   147→        """Get all versions for a (name, scope) pair."""
   148→        key = self._scope_key(name, scope)
   149→        return self._configs.get(key, [])
   150→
   151→    def create_version(
   152→        self,
   153→        name: str,
   154→        scope: Scope,
   155→        config: dict[str, Any],
   156→        includes: list[IncludeRef],
   157→        inherits_active: bool = False
   158→    ) -> ConfigVersion:
   159→        """Create a new immutable version."""
   160→        key = self._scope_key(name, scope)
   161→        versions = self._configs.get(key, [])
   162→
   163→        # Check max versions
   164→        if len(versions) >= MAX_VERSIONS_PER_SCOPE:
   165→            raise ConflictError('conflict', f'Maximum {MAX_VERSIONS_PER_SCOPE} versions reached for {name}')
   166→
   167→        # Determine next version number
   168→        next_version = len(versions) + 1
   169→
   170→        # Check idempotency - if identical request, return existing
   171→        # Two configs are identical if same name, scope, config, and includes
   172→        for v in versions:
   173→            if v.config == config and v.includes == tuple(includes):
   174→                # Return existing version, but ensure it's active
   175→                if not v.active:
   176→                    # Need to activate it
   177→                    object.__setattr__(v, 'active', True)
   178→                return v
   179→
   180→        # If inherits_active is True, inherit omitted fields from active version
   181→        if inherits_active and versions:
   182→            active = versions[-1]  # Last one is active
   183→            # Deep merge active.config into config (active wins for conflicts)
   184→            config = deep_merge(active.config, config)
   185→
   186→        # Deactivate all existing versions
   187→        for v in versions:
   188→            object.__setattr__(v, 'active', False)
   189→
   190→        # Create new version
   191→        new_version = ConfigVersion(
   192→            name=name,
   193→            scope=scope,
   194→            version=next_version,
   195→            config=config,
   196→            includes=tuple(includes),
   197→            active=True
   198→        )
   199→
   200→        # Store it
   201→        self._configs[key] = versions + [new_version]
   202→
   203→        return new_version
   204→
   205→    def get_version(self, name: str, scope: Scope, version: int) -> ConfigVersion:
   206→        """Get a specific version."""
   207→        versions = self._get_versions(name, scope)
   208→        for v in versions:
   209→            if v.version == version:
   210→                return v
   211→        raise NotFoundError('not_found', f'Version {version} not found for {name}')
   212→
   213→    def get_active(self, name: str, scope: Scope) -> ConfigVersion:
   214→        """Get the active version."""
   215→        versions = self._get_versions(name, scope)
   216→        for v in reversed(versions):
   217→            if v.active:
   218→                return v
   219→        raise NotFoundError('not_found', f'No active version for {name}')
   220→
   221→    def list_versions(self, name: str, scope: Scope) -> list[ConfigVersion]:
   222→        """List all versions for a (name, scope) pair."""
   223→        return list(self._get_versions(name, scope))
   224→
   225→    def activate_version(self, name: str, scope: Scope, version: int) -> ConfigVersion:
   226→        """Activate a specific version."""
   227→        versions = self._get_versions(name, scope)
   228→
   229→        target = None
   230→        for v in versions:
   231→            if v.version == version:
   232→                target = v
   233→                break
   234→
   235→        if target is None:
   236→            raise NotFoundError('not_found', f'Version {version} not found for {name}')
   237→
   238→        # Deactivate all others
   239→        for v in versions:
   240→            object.__setattr__(v, 'active', v.version == version)
   241→
   242→        return target
   243→
   244→    def rollback(self, name: str, scope: Scope, to_version: int) -> ConfigVersion:
   245→        """Rollback to an earlier version."""
   246→        versions = self._get_versions(name, scope)
   247→
   248→        # Check if to_version exists
   249→        target = None
   250→        for v in versions:
   251→            if v.version == to_version:
   252→                target = v
   253→                break
   254→
   255→        if target is None:
   256→            raise NotFoundError('not_found', f'Version {to_version} not found for {name}')
   257→
   258→        # Find current active
   259→        current_active = None
   260→        for v in reversed(versions):
   261→            if v.active:
   262→                current_active = v
   263→                break
   264→
   265→        if current_active is None:
   266→            raise NotFoundError('not_found', f'No active version for {name}')
   267→
   268→        # Only allow rollback to earlier or same version
   269→        if to_version > current_active.version:
   270→            raise ConflictError('conflict', f'Cannot rollback to version {to_version} (newer than active {current_active.version})')
   271→
   272→        return self.activate_version(name, scope, to_version)
   273→

   275→# =============================================================================
   276→# Deep merge with type checking
   277→# =============================================================================
   278→
   279→def deep_merge(base: dict[str, Any], override: dict[str, Any], path: str = '') -> dict[str, Any]:
   280→    """
   281→    Deep merge two dictionaries.
   282→    Override values take precedence.
   283→    Raises UnprocessableError on type conflicts.
   284→    """
   285→    result = dict(base)
   286→
   287→    for key, value in override.items():
   288→        current_path = f'{path}/{key}' if path else f'/{key}'
   289→
   290→        if key in result:
   291→            existing = result[key]
   292→
   293→            # Type conflict detection
   294→            if isinstance(existing, dict) and isinstance(value, dict):
   295→                # Both are dicts, recurse
   296→                result[key] = deep_merge(existing, value, current_path)
   297→            elif isinstance(existing, list) and isinstance(value, list):
   298→                # Both are lists, replace entirely (right wins)
   299→                result[key] = value
   300→            elif isinstance(existing, (str, int, float, bool, type(None))) and \
   301→                 isinstance(value, (str, int, float, bool, type(None))):
   302→                # Both are scalars, replace (right wins)
   303→                result[key] = value
   304→            else:
   305→                # Type conflict
   306→                raise UnprocessableError(
   307→                    'unprocessable',
   308→                    f'Type conflict at {current_path}: {type(existing).__name__} vs {type(value).__name__}',

def resolve_config(
    storage: ConfigStorage,
    name: str,
    scope: Scope,
    version: int | None,
    visited: set[tuple[str, int, int]] | None = None
) -> tuple[dict[str, Any], list[ResolutionNode]]:
    """
    Resolve a config with all includes applied.

    Returns (resolved_config, resolution_graph).

    Raises:
        NotFoundError: If referenced config doesn't exist.
        CycleDetectedError: If a cycle is detected.
        UnprocessableError: If merge has type conflicts.
    """
    if visited is None:
        visited = set()

    # Check max depth
    if len(visited) > MAX_INCLUDE_CHAIN:
        raise UnprocessableError(
            'unprocessable',
            'Maximum include chain length exceeded',
            {'reason': 'max_depth'}
        )

    # Get the config version
    if version is None:
        config_version = storage.get_active(name, scope)
    else:
        config_version = storage.get_version(name, scope, version)

    # Create unique identifier for this config/version
    config_id = (name, hash(scope), config_version.version)

    # Check for cycles
    if config_id in visited:
        raise CycleDetectedError(
            'cycle_detected',
            f'Cycle detected involving {name}',
            {'cycle': [n.to_dict() for n in visited]}
        )

    visited = visited | {config_id}

    # Start with empty object
    resolved = {}
    graph = [ResolutionNode(
        name=config_version.name,
        scope=config_version.scope,
        version_used=config_version.version
    )]

    # Process includes in order
    for include_ref in config_version.includes:
        # Determine which version to use
        ref_version = include_ref.version
        if ref_version is None:
            # Use active version at resolution time
            ref_version = storage.get_active(include_ref.name, include_ref.scope).version

        # Recursively resolve
        included_config, included_graph = resolve_config(
            storage,
            include_ref.name,
            include_ref.scope,
            ref_version,
            visited
        )

        # Merge into accumulator
        try:
            resolved = deep_merge(resolved, included_config)
        except UnprocessableError as e:
            # Re-raise with more context
            raise UnprocessableError(
                'unprocessable',
                e.message,
                e.details
            )

def resolve_config(
    storage: ConfigStorage,
    name: str,
    scope: Scope,
    version: int | None,
    visited: set[tuple[str, int, int]] | None = None
) -> tuple[dict[str, Any], list[ResolutionNode]]:
    """
    Resolve a config with all includes applied.

    Returns (resolved_config, resolution_graph).

    Raises:
        NotFoundError: If referenced config doesn't exist.
        CycleDetectedError: If a cycle is detected.
        UnprocessableError: If merge has type conflicts.
    """
    if visited is None:
        visited = set()

    # Check max depth
    if len(visited) > MAX_INCLUDE_CHAIN:
        raise UnprocessableError(
            'unprocessable',
            'Maximum include chain length exceeded',
            {'reason': 'max_depth'}
        )

    # Get the config version
    if version is None:
        config_version = storage.get_active(name, scope)
    else:
        config_version = storage.get_version(name, scope, version)

    # Create unique identifier for this config/version
    config_id = (name, hash(scope), config_version.version)

    # Check for cycles
    if config_id in visited:
        raise CycleDetectedError(
            'cycle_detected',
            f'Cycle detected involving {name}',
            {'cycle': [n.to_dict() for n in visited]}
        )

    visited = visited | {config_id}

    # Start with empty object
    resolved = {}
    graph = [ResolutionNode(
        name=config_version.name,
        scope=config_version.scope,
        version_used=config_version.version
    )]

    # Process includes in order
    for include_ref in config_version.includes:
        # Determine which version to use
        ref_version = include_ref.version
        if ref_version is None:
            # Use active version at resolution time
            ref_version = storage.get_active(include_ref.name, include_ref.scope).version

        # Recursively resolve
        included_config, included_graph = resolve_config(
            storage,
            include_ref.name,
            include_ref.scope,
            ref_version,
            visited
        )

        # Merge into accumulator
        try:
            resolved = deep_merge(resolved, included_config)
        except UnprocessableError as e:
            # Re-raise with more context
            raise UnprocessableError(
                'unprocessable',
                e.message,
                e.details
            )

        # Add to graph (without duplicates)
        for node in included_graph:
            node_id = (node.name, hash(node.scope), node.version_used)
            if node_id not in { (n.name, hash(n.scope), n.version_used) for n in graph }:
                graph.append(node)

    # Finally, merge own config on top
    try:
        resolved = deep_merge(resolved, config_version.config)
    except UnprocessableError as e:
        raise UnprocessableError(
            'unprocessable',
            e.message,
            e.details
        )

    return resolved, graph
